project: VOLT OLT SFP Stick — Reverse Engineering & Protocol Research

protocol.name: VOLT Management Protocol
protocol.ethertype_description: IEEE 802.1 Local Experimental EtherType 2 (public use, not registered)
protocol.transport: Raw Ethernet broadcast, no IP, no VLAN required

discovery_cycle.description: VOLT tool sends continuous Get requests in a repeating cycle
cycle_pattern: OnuStatus queries (0x0201/0x0209) for each ONU slot 0x00-0x1F

sfp_eeprom.note: EEPROM is spoofed/generic — reports as 850nm copper SFP despite being a 1310/1490nm GPON OLT

closest_match.name: VOLTHA Tibit OLT Adapter
closest_match.relevance: HIGH — same architecture (Scapy L2 to SFP OLT stick) but different protocol

differences.vlan: 4090 with prio 7 vs no VLAN required
differences.protocol: EOAM/DPoE TLVs vs proprietary magic+msg_id format

other_references.name: VOLTHA EPON OLT Adapter (Go)
other_references.relevance: MEDIUM — Go implementation for Tibit, different protocol

other_references.name: fs_xgspon_mod
other_references.relevance: MEDIUM — raw L2 socket pattern for SFP stick management

other_references.name: Hack GPON
other_references.relevance: MEDIUM — community wiki for GPON stick hacking

commercial_alternatives.name: Tibit MCMS
commercial_alternatives.note: Standard IEEE 1904.2, not compatible with VOLT protocol

commercial_alternatives.name: Exaware
commercial_alternatives.note: Commercial PON management OS with Tibit integration

commercial_alternatives.name: FS PON Manager
commercial_alternatives.note: Management app for FS.com OLT SFP sticks, protocol unknown

ieee_1904_2.standard_name: IEEE 1904.2 — Standard for Control and Management of EPON

experimental_ethertypes.0x88B6: IEEE 802.1 Local Experimental EtherType 2 (used by VOLT)

ieee_1904_2.volt_relationship: VOLT uses 0x88B6 (experimental) instead of 0xA8C8 (standard). Custom frame format instead of EOAM/DPoE TLVs. No VLAN tagging required. Likely a proprietary Chinese implementation inspired by IEEE 1904.2 concepts.

mikrotik_integration.confirmed_working: MikroTik Hex S (1G SFP) — from video tutorials

sfp_settings.auto_negotiation: no (must be disabled on SFP+ ports)
sfp_settings.note: CRS354 and CCR2004 SFP+ ports need forced 1G-baseX. Simple 1G SFP ports auto-negotiate fine.

mikrotik_integration.bridge_config: SFP port + Ethernet ports in same bridge, VOLT tool on any bridged Ethernet port
mikrotik_integration.thermal: OLT SFP runs hot, 95C shutdown threshold, heatsink recommended

defective_units.symptoms: link-ok, valid EEPROM, normal optical power, but zero management response
tested_on: CRS354-48G-4S+2Q+ (various bridge configs, HW offload disabled)
CCR2004-1G-12S+2XS (pure CPU bridging, no switch ASIC)
Second CCR2004-1G-12S+2XS (to rule out host device)
defective_units.conclusion: Both units confirmed defective — management firmware non-responsive

replacement_orders: 2x VOLT-32 OLT SFP sticks (replacement, ETA 1-2 days)
TP-Link MC220L media converter (simplest test setup)
FS.com GPON-SFP-OLT-MAC-I (128 ONU, Class C+, separate vendor)
